title: Pre-Ecolier Practice Set 17 — Logic and Puzzles
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official competition questions.

questions.question_text: Look at the pattern: apple, banana, apple, banana, apple, ? What comes next?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='12'/><ellipse cx='60' cy='30' rx='14' ry='6'/><circle cx='100' cy='30' r='12'/><ellipse cx='140' cy='30' rx='14' ry='6'/><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='12'/><rect x='210' y='18' width='24' height='24' fill='#ffffff'/><text x='218' y='36' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A row showing apple, banana, apple, banana, apple, and a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates apple and banana. After apple comes banana.

questions.question_text: Maya has 4 red marbles and 3 blue marbles. How many marbles does she have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='45' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='140' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='165' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='190' cy='30' r='10'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four marbles on the left and three marbles on the right.
questions.solution: 4 plus 3 equals 7 marbles.

questions.question_text: Which animal lives in the water?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><ellipse cx='30' cy='35' rx='20' ry='12'/><rect x='70' y='25' width='30' height='25'/><ellipse cx='140' cy='35' rx='22' ry='10'/><rect x='180' y='20' width='30' height='30'/><circle cx='240' cy='35' r='12'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Row of five different animal shapes.
questions.solution: Fish live in the water.

questions.question_text: How many apples are on the tree?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='40'/><rect x='75' y='85' width='10' height='30'/><circle cx='60' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='80' cy='35' r='6'/><circle cx='100' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='70' cy='60' r='6'/><circle cx='90' cy='60' r='6'/></svg>
questions.solution: There are 5 apples on the tree.

questions.question_text: Tom has more candies than Lily. Lily has more candies than Mia. Who has the fewest candies?
questions.solution: Tom more than Lily more than Mia, so Mia has the fewest.

questions.question_text: What number comes next: 1, 3, 5, 7, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><text x='20' y='32' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>1</text><rect x='50' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><text x='60' y='32' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>3</text><rect x='90' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><text x='100' y='32' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>5</text><rect x='130' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><text x='140' y='32' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>7</text><rect x='170' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><text x='180' y='32' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Sequence of boxes with numbers 1, 3, 5, 7, and a question mark.
questions.solution: The numbers go up by 2 each time. 7 plus 2 is 9.

questions.question_text: There are 10 birds on a fence. 4 fly away. How many are left?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><line x1='0' y1='45' x2='260' y2='45' stroke='currentColor'/><ellipse cx='20' cy='35' rx='8' ry='5'/><ellipse cx='50' cy='35' rx='8' ry='5'/><ellipse cx='80' cy='35' rx='8' ry='5'/><ellipse cx='110' cy='35' rx='8' ry='5'/><ellipse cx='140' cy='35' rx='8' ry='5'/><ellipse cx='170' cy='35' rx='8' ry='5'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Six birds sitting on a fence after four flew away.
questions.solution: 10 minus 4 equals 6 birds remaining.

questions.question_text: Emma has a shape with 4 equal sides and 4 right angles. What shape is it?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='15' y='15' width='50' height='50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A shape with 4 equal sides and 4 right angles.
questions.solution: Four equal sides and four right angles describe a square.

questions.question_text: Ella, Finn, and Grace each have one pet: a cat, a dog, or a rabbit. Ella's pet has long ears. Finn's pet barks. What is Grace's pet?
questions.solution: Long ears means Ella has the rabbit. Barks means Finn has the dog. So Grace has the cat.

questions.question_text: The clock now shows 3:00. What time will it be in 2 hours?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='75' y2='50' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: 3 plus 2 is 5. The time will be 5:00.

questions.question_text: Shape pattern: heart, moon, heart, moon, heart, moon, heart. What is the 8th shape?
questions.image_description: Alternating hearts and moons with a question mark at position 8.
questions.solution: Odd positions are hearts, even positions are moons. Position 8 is even, so it is a moon.

questions.question_text: A farmer has 3 cows and 5 chickens. How many legs are there in total?
questions.solution: 3 cows have 12 legs, 5 chickens have 10 legs. 12 plus 10 equals 22.

questions.question_text: Three boxes are colored red, green, and yellow. The ball is not in the red box. The ball is not in the yellow box. Where is the ball?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='130' y='10' width='40' height='40'/></svg>
questions.solution: Since the ball is not in red or yellow, it must be in the green box.

questions.question_text: Put these numbers in order from smallest to largest: 7, 2, 9, 4. Which is the second number in order?
questions.solution: In order: 2, 4, 7, 9. The second number is 4.

questions.question_text: Look at the picture of a house. How many windows does it have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='60' width='120' height='70'/><polygon points='20,60 80,20 140,60'/><rect x='40' y='75' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='75' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='100' y='75' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='100' width='20' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A house with a triangular roof, a door, and windows.
questions.solution: The house has 3 windows in a row.

questions.question_text: Lucas has 12 stickers. He gives 3 to his sister and 4 to his friend. How many stickers does he have left?
questions.solution: 12 minus 3 equals 9, minus 4 equals 5 stickers left.

questions.question_text: Four friends line up. Max is not first and not last. Zoe is last. Leo is right before Max. Who is first?
questions.solution: Zoe is 4th. Max is 2nd or 3rd. If Max is 2nd, Leo is 1st and there is no place for Ivy before Zoe. So Max is 3rd, Leo is 2nd, and Ivy is first.

questions.question_text: How many shaded cells are in the checkerboard grid below?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='#ffffff'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='#ffffff'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='#ffffff'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='#ffffff'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='30' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 grid with a checkerboard pattern.
questions.solution: The checkerboard 3x3 pattern has 5 shaded cells: the 4 corners and the center.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 2 coins. An eraser costs 3 coins. Nora buys 2 pencils and 1 eraser. How many coins does she pay?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='20' width='50' height='10'/><rect x='70' y='20' width='50' height='10'/><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='15'/></svg>
questions.solution: 2 pencils cost 4 coins, 1 eraser costs 3 coins. Total is 7 coins.

questions.question_text: A frog sits on stone 1 of stones numbered 1 to 9. It jumps 2 stones forward each time. Which stone is it on after 3 jumps?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='50' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='80' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='110' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='140' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='170' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='200' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='230' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='260' cy='25' r='12'/></svg>
questions.solution: Start at 1. Jump to 3, then 5, then 7. After 3 jumps, the frog is on stone 7.

questions.question_text: Three children each wear one hat: red, blue, or green. Kate does not wear red. Liam wears blue. What color does Mia wear?
questions.solution: Liam wears blue. Kate does not wear red, so Kate wears green. Mia must wear red.

questions.question_text: A box contains 3 red balls and 2 yellow balls. Without looking, what is the smallest number of balls you must take out to be sure you have at least one red ball?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='140' height='40'/><circle cx='35' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='85' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='135' cy='30' r='8'/></svg>
questions.solution: In the worst case you pick both yellow balls first. The 3rd ball must be red. So you need 3 balls.

questions.question_text: A caterpillar colors groups of dots: 1 red, 2 blue, 3 red, 4 blue, 5 red. What color is the 6th group?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='15' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='40' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='55' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='85' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='100' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='115' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='145' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='160' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='175' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='190' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='220' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='235' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='250' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='265' cy='25' r='6'/><circle cx='280' cy='25' r='6'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Groups of dots arranged along a caterpillar.
questions.solution: Odd groups are red, even groups are blue. Group 6 is even, so it is blue.

questions.question_text: Four cards show the numbers 2, 4, 6, 8. How many different pairs of cards have a sum of 10?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><text x='22' y='36' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>2</text><rect x='60' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><text x='72' y='36' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>4</text><rect x='110' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><text x='122' y='36' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>6</text><rect x='160' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><text x='172' y='36' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='#000'>8</text></svg>
questions.solution: Pairs that sum to 10: 2 and 8, 4 and 6. That is 2 pairs.
